title: psorospermum laxiflorum
intro: Psorospermum laxiflorum, also known as the Quinine Tree or Cinchona tree, is a plant species native to Madagascar and Southeast Asia. This tree is a source of the alkaloid quinine, which has been used for centuries to treat malaria and other fever-related illnesses due to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Psorospermum laxiflorum include reducing fever, alleviating symptoms of malaria, and exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Psorospermum laxiflorum include quinine-infused water, tablets, and capsules, which are often used to prevent and treat malaria, as well as other conditions such as altitude sickness.
